ibis.orig: the long-term impact of COVID-19 on doctors
ibis.text: What is the long-term impact of COVID-19 on doctors?

ibis.orig: significant personal and professional impact of long covid
ibis.text: Long covid has a significant personal and professional impact on doctors.

ibis.orig: 18% of survey respondents reported that they were left unable to work, and others have reduced their working and/or training hours
ibis.text: 18% of survey respondents reported being unable to work, and others reduced their working or training hours.

ibis.orig: The BMA has called for a range of measures, including financial support and improved services for doctors and healthcare staff affected by long covid
ibis.text: The BMA argues that financial support and improved services should be provided for doctors and healthcare staff affected by long covid.

ibis.orig: The BMA has also called for the condition to be recognised as an occupational disease for healthcare workers
ibis.text: Long covid should be recognised as an occupational disease for healthcare workers.

ibis.orig: the widespread impact that long covid was having on the workforce
ibis.text: What is the widespread impact that long covid is having on the workforce?

ibis.orig: Long COVID symptoms that seem to have the greatest impact on work and return to work are fatigue, cognitive dysfunction (such as difficulty concentrating and memory loss), and changes in taste and smell
ibis.text: Fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, and changes in taste and smell are the symptoms with the greatest impact on work and returning to work.

ibis.orig: one in four employers now include Long COVID among their main causes of long-term sickness absence
ibis.text: One in four employers include long covid as a main cause of long-term sickness absence.

ibis.orig: Return to work (RTW) after prolonged sickness absence with Long COVID needs a tailored, long-term and flexible approach
ibis.text: Returning to work after prolonged sickness absence with long covid requires a tailored, long-term, and flexible approach.

ibis.orig: A key difference between Long COVID and other conditions is the multifactorial nature of the condition and it is important to distinguish the specific health problems in each case
ibis.text: A tailored approach is necessary because long covid is multifactorial, unlike other conditions, making it important to distinguish specific health problems in each case.

ibis.orig: working-age people are less likely to participate in the labour market after developing long COVID symptoms than they were before being infected with coronavirus (COVID-19)
ibis.text: Working-age people are less likely to participate in the labour market after developing long covid symptoms than before their infection.

ibis.orig: the potential impact of long covid on the labour market
ibis.text: What is the potential impact of long covid on the labour market?

ibis.orig: the impact of Long covid was equivalent to 110,000 workers being off sick on any given day
ibis.text: The impact of long covid is equivalent to 110,000 workers being off sick on any given day.

ibis.orig: long covid was unlikely to be driving the increase in economic inactivity
ibis.text: Long covid is unlikely to be driving the increase in economic inactivity.

ibis.orig: it would be observed in other countries with high rates of infection during the pandemic
ibis.text: If long covid drove economic inactivity, this trend would be observed in other countries with high infection rates.

ibis.orig: The impact of long COVID-19 and NHS waiting lists on economic inactivity is unclear
ibis.text: The impact of long covid and NHS waiting lists on economic inactivity is unclear.
